Feature_Name: L1/L2 Triggered Mobility (LTM) Configuration via RRCReconfiguration

3GPP_Reference.Title: NR; Radio Resource Control (RRC); Protocol Specification, NG-RAN; Architecture and functional description

Feature_Description: The gNB-CU prepares an 'RRCReconfiguration' RRC message containing LTM Configuration Information Elements (IEs). This LTM configuration data is based on information received in previous F1AP messages. The 'RRCReconfiguration' message buffer is then piggybacked within an F1AP 'DL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER' message and sent to the source gNB-DU. The source gNB-DU is responsible for forwarding this 'RRCReconfiguration' message to the User Equipment (UE).

Upon successful reception and application of the 'RRCReconfiguration' message, the UE responds with an 'RRCReconfigurationComplete' RRC message. This response message is sent to the source gNB-DU, which then forwards it to the gNB-CU, piggybacked in an F1AP 'UL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER' message.
Feature_Objectives: To configure the UE with parameters for L1/L2 Triggered Mobility (LTM).
To enable the gNB-CU to convey LTM configuration data to the UE via RRC signalling, utilizing the F1AP interface for transport between gNB-CU and gNB-DU.
To ensure the UE acknowledges the successful application of the LTM configuration.

RRC_Scope.Direction: Downlink (gNB-CU to UE), Uplink (UE to gNB-CU)

RRC_States_And_Transitions.Trigger: gNB-CU decides to initiate LTM configuration based on UE measurement reports.
RRC_States_And_Transitions.Impact: UE applies new LTM configuration, potentially affecting its mobility behavior within or between gNB-DUs.

RRC_Messages.Purpose: To configure the UE with L1/L2 Triggered Mobility (LTM) parameters.
To acknowledge the successful reception and application of the RRCReconfiguration message by the UE.
To transfer the RRCReconfiguration message from the gNB-CU to the gNB-DU over the F1-C interface.
To transfer the RRCReconfigurationComplete message from the gNB-DU to the gNB-CU over the F1-C interface.

RRC_Procedures.Trigger_Condition: The gNB-CU determines to initiate LTM configuration based on L3 MeasurementReport messages received from the UE.
RRC_Procedures.Description: This procedure is used for LTM when the UE moves within the same gNB-DU during NR operation. The gNB-CU sends a UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST message to the gNB-DU for each candidate cell, containing a candidate cell ID and CSI resource configuration for LTM. The gNB-DU responds with a UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE. Subsequently, the gNB-CU sends a DL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER message, including the RRCReconfiguration message with LTM configuration, to the gNB-DU. The gNB-DU forwards this RRCReconfiguration message to the UE. The UE responds with an RRCReconfigurationComplete message to the gNB-DU, which then forwards it to the gNB-CU via an UL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER message.

RRC_Procedures.Trigger_Condition: The gNB-CU determines to initiate LTM configuration based on L3 MeasurementReport messages received from the UE.
RRC_Procedures.Description: This procedure is used for LTM when the UE moves from one gNB-DU to another gNB-DU within the same gNB-CU during NR operation. The gNB-CU sends a UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message to the candidate gNB-DU(s) for each candidate cell, containing a candidate cell ID and CSI resource configuration for LTM. The candidate gNB-DU responds with a UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE. The gNB-CU also sends a UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST to the source gNB-DU. Subsequently, the gNB-CU sends a DL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER message, including the RRCReconfiguration message with LTM configuration, to the source gNB-DU. The source gNB-DU forwards this RRCReconfiguration message to the UE. The UE responds with an RRCReconfigurationComplete message to the source gNB-DU, which then forwards it to the gNB-CU via an UL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER message.

Security_And_Context.Security_Mode_Impact: Once AS security is activated, all RRC messages on SRB1, SRB2, SRB3, SRB4 and SRB5, including those containing NAS messages, are integrity protected and ciphered by PDCP. RRCReconfiguration and RRCReconfigurationComplete messages are RRC messages and would thus be integrity protected and ciphered.
Security_And_Context.UE_Context_Changes: The gNB-CU sends UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST (for intra-gNB-DU LTM) or UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST (for inter-gNB-DU LTM) messages to the gNB-DU(s) to prepare or modify the UE context, including candidate cell IDs and CSI resource configurations for LTM. The gNB-DU(s) respond with UE CONTEXT SETUP/MODIFICATION RESPONSE messages, including generated lower layer RRC configurations.

Interlayer_Interactions.Purpose: To encapsulate and transfer the RRCReconfiguration message from gNB-CU to gNB-DU.
To encapsulate and transfer the RRCReconfigurationComplete message from gNB-DU to gNB-CU.
To configure the UE with LTM parameters.
To acknowledge the successful application of RRCReconfiguration by the UE.

Procedure_Implementation_Steps: 1. The gNB-CU sends a DL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER message to the source gNB-DU, which includes the generated RRCReconfiguration message with the LTM configuration.
2. The source gNB-DU forwards the received RRCReconfiguration message to the UE.
3. The UE responds to the source gNB-DU with an RRCReconfigurationComplete message.
4. The source gNB-DU forwards the RRCReconfigurationComplete message to the gNB-CU via UL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER message.
